scope.summary: Review fix: the C6 classifier-split close (bp8ia.4.6.4) had its evidence source.sha misbound to 984de104 (a later unrelated ratchet commit) instead of b233fff0 (the commit that actually contains the split). Corrected the binding and re-verified the underlying isomorphism claim before re-closing.
out_of_scope: The classifier split code itself (b233fff0) — unchanged; only its evidence binding was wrong.
The shared ratchet script bookkeeping (documented in the corrected evidence's trace, not part of the isomorphism claim).
Any change to the guard, classifier, fuzz target, or test expectations.

entry_points.name: Diagnosis of the misbinding
entry_points.trace: git show --stat b233fff0 confirms it is the split commit (5 paths: classifier.rs -140, classifier_grammar.rs +140, lib.rs +2, fuzz/Cargo.lock, arch_fitness_lint.sh). The original close bound source=984de104 ('chore: lower dispatch file-size ratchet'), a later unrelated commit. The evidence file's own limitations note had admitted the source was 'current reproducible HEAD after later unrelated pane commits' — the misbinding.

entry_points.name: Re-verification that the split WAS isomorphic (the critical check)
entry_points.trace: Before re-binding, re-ran the three isomorphism proofs on the split code, all passing unchanged: (1) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard --test adversarial_corpus = 15/15 (incl. strict_corpus_is_never_underclassified, corpus_is_never_underclassified, classifier_never_panics_on_arbitrary_input); (2) cargo test -p oraclemcp-guard = 319 unit + all integration tests, 0 failures; (3) cargo +nightly-2026-05-11 fuzz run classify_fuzz -runs=256 -seed=4001 = DONE, no crash/assertion. Additionally diffed the 4 guard-crate split files between b233fff0 and HEAD: byte-for-byte IDENTICAL (no drift). Conclusion: the split was isomorphic; only the evidence source binding was wrong — NOT a non-isomorphic split.

entry_points.name: Binding correction + re-close
entry_points.trace: Corrected evidence source.sha 984de104 -> b233fff0 (commit 1fed082f), moved the shared ratchet script out of the source-binding integrity surface (it drifts across sibling C6 splits) while keeping it verified in the trace, then ran scripts/bead_tracker_guard.sh correct-false-close --original-bead oraclemcp-eng-program-bp8ia.4.6.4 which reopened and re-closed with source=b233fff0. scripts/check_bead_close_evidence.sh now reports 0 hard findings for bp8ia.4.6.4 (was 3: CLOSE_REASON_SOURCE_MISMATCH, CLOSE_EVIDENCE_CHANGED_AFTER_CLOSE, SCOPE_CHANGED_AFTER_SOURCE).

limitations: This is an evidence-honesty repair, not a code change. The classifier split code at b233fff0 was not modified.
The re-verification used the bounded local cargo-fuzz smoke (256 runs), matching the original evidence's basis; no hosted scheduled fuzz campaign is claimed.
